key_insight: Raising cold-spray pressure from 30 to 60 bar increases copper particle velocity above the bonding threshold, producing denser, more ductile deposits with better conductivity but lower hardness.
engineering_relevance: For CSAM copper parts, spray pressure is a key process lever to balance electrical performance and mechanical integrity, especially in applications needing conductive structural builds.
method: Cold-sprayed copper with a LightSPEE3D system at 30, 40, 50, and 60 bar.
Measured XRD, hardness, eddy-current conductivity, tensile properties, and SEM microstructure.
Compared how pressure changes particle velocity, bonding, and deformation mechanisms.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports pressure-dependent XRD refinement, higher conductivity, lower hardness, and a clear transition toward stronger, more ductile tensile behavior at higher pressures.
watch_item: The apparent optimum at higher pressure may depend on material, stand-off distance, and system-specific velocity profiles, so the window may not transfer directly to other CSAM setups.
paper_title: Optimising Cold Spray Additive Manufacturing: Pressure-driven enhancement of mechanical performance in copper deposits
